_label_note: SA Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered Secretariat role-mailbox bait, public/label-only phone bait, CR/VAT/National Address/Iqama numeric fragments, URL CR/NID/Iqama fragments, Hijri Sha-ban date bait, public MOU wording, and training-roster insider-list wording. Generated placeholder/invalid KSA National ID, Iqama, and CR labels were removed from strict must_detect when current deterministic strict mode suppresses them.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

must_detect.matched_text: As issuer preparing RGX listing, we note that draft prospectus sections and pricing scenarios constitute MNPI under KCMC market‑conduct guidance.
Non‑public figures currently under review include a provisional IPO price range of SAR 18–22 and unaudited Q2 EBITDA of SAR 47.3m; disclosure prior to the RGX filing is prohibited.

Confidential Board Compliance Memo: Red Sand Industries JSC (CR RS-88-4455667) — PDPL and Market Conduct Update.
As issuer preparing RGX listing, we note that draft prospectus sections and pricing scenarios constitute MNPI under KCMC market‑conduct guidance.
Non‑public figures currently under review include a provisional IPO price range of SAR 18–22 and unaudited Q2 EBITDA of SAR 47.3m; disclosure prior to the RGX filing is prohibited.
The Market Conduct Unit also flagged the internal “OryxPay 3.0” launch metrics and a confidential customer offboarding as price‑sensitive until formal announcements.
Personal data processing: Mr. Sami Al‑Hazmi (Iqama ID QP-593-04-888, employee ID RSI-004217) provided iris and fingerprint templates for access control and disclosed treatment for Type 1 diabetes during benefits enrollment.
Mr. Al‑Hazmi’s HR file also records his voluntary affiliation with the Al‑Wifaq Workers Union and his declared religious affiliation for holiday scheduling; processing requires a heightened lawful basis and access controls per PDPL.
In addition, dependent data for Ms. Al‑Hazmi’s 14‑year‑old daughter (minor beneficiary ID DEP-77-114) is in scope, and no transfer may occur without parental consent and Data Transfer Impact Assessment.
Cross‑border transfer of HR archives to the approved cloud in Bahrara City awaits Supreme Data and Automation Authority (SDAA) approval; no exports should occur before the SDAA ticket SDAA-TX-2194 is cleared.
For avoidance of doubt, the politics of pricing strategy and general religion references in marketing copy are out of scope for special‑category assessment where they do not identify a person.
Insider list must include Dr. Laila Al‑Harbi (CFO) and Eng. Nasser Al‑Mansour (Head of IT Security) as of 12:00 AST today; a trading blackout applies to
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_not_detect.reason: Product name; not PII/MNPI under rules.
Metaphorical use; not an individual's political opinion.
Generic discussion; not tied to an identifiable person.
Negated processing note; not data about an identifiable individual here.

uncertain.reason: Likely an insurance or benefits member ID; no explicit allowed rule fits (e.g., insurance_member_id ambiguous).
Consent is a privacy-event marker, but only withdrawal is enumerated in allowed rules.
Transfer-impact assessment is a cross-border privacy control, but not an explicitly listed rule.
Internal ticket identifier relevant to cross-border approval; no matching allowed rule.
